codeReview.summary: The fix resolves 4 of the 5 prior blocking findings (vLLM panic path, PID=0 fallback, status backend omission, and starting/stopping status regression). However, swap drain handling is still unsafe on error paths: drain is set before stop but not cleared if backend creation fails, which can leave chat permanently returning 503 after recovery attempts.

issues.description: post_swap() sets draining=true before the drain sleep, but if swap fails before backend replacement (e.g. create_backend returns Err for vLLM at line 273), draining is never reset. The function returns 500 on line 355 without clearing drain, while post_chat rejects whenever is_draining() is true (line 592). This can leave the daemon stuck rejecting chat requests even after later starts, so prior scrutiny issue #5 is not fully resolved on failure paths.

The inference canary subscribes to subscribe_errors() once at startup. After backend replacement during swap (routes.rs line 281), this receiver is stale for immediate error-triggered checks on the new backend. Periodic canary still runs, but event-driven CUDA error triggers from the new backend are missed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Swap-drain guidance/tests emphasize success-path behavior but do not explicitly require cleanup assertions on swap failure paths.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md VAL-CROSS-007 describes successful swap flow, and AGENTS.md API contract guidance focuses response-shape tests; neither explicitly covers asserting drain reset when swap errors before backend replacement.

summary: Fix review failed: four prior blockers are resolved, but swap drain cleanup is still unsafe on error paths and can leave chat permanently unavailable (503). A prior non-blocking canary-subscription gap also remains.
